mechanism_narrative: A flat or declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount-rate pressure on equities and supports multiple expansion, so lower short-term policy rates predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are not higher when FEDFUNDS is falling or below its 6-month average, or if walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above its recent trend, real rates and expected policy restraint rise, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-crossing episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than negative ones, or if the sign of the signal is unstable across two consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income growth and slower demand, which lowers earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if unemployment-rise episodes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of cases, or if the conditional median return is not below the unconditional median by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: Joint labor-market deterioration, captured by falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE, identifies a demand shock stronger than either series alone and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up condition does not outperform either variable alone in predicting negative 20-day returns, or if the interaction term is not statistically negative in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal final demand and better near-term revenue growth for index constituents, so retail sales strength should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if periods of rising RSAFS do not correspond to above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign flips negative for two or more consecutive 1-year subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and HOUST both weaken, the economy is moving into a synchronized slowdown that reduces cyclical earnings and raises recession risk, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is negative and HOUST 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in INDPRO and HOUST is not followed by materially lower 60-day returns than when only one of the two weakens, or if the interaction effect is positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 confirms real activity is decelerating, which should lower earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if quarterly GDPC1 contractions are not followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than expansions, or if the directional hit rate falls below 50% in any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A threshold break in INDPRO relative to its recent trend signals a manufacturing rebound that improves earnings momentum and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average after at least 2 consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if manufacturing rebound triggers are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the average 5-day return after the trigger is indistinguishable from zero across walk-forward samples.
